entry.title: Domino's Debuts New Spicy Chicken Bacon Ranch Pizza
entry.summary:
Domino's debuts the new Spicy Chicken Bacon Ranch Pizza as the latest addition to its Specialty Pizza lineup.
The pizza comes topped with grilled chicken breast, ranch, bacon, jalapenos, provolone, cheese made with mozzarella (AKA pizza cheese), and a drizzle of Buffalo sauce. The chain is also offering a new $9.99 mix-and-match deal on medium Specialty Pizzas, including the new Spicy Chicken Bacon Ranch Pizza. Domino's offers 10 other Specialty Pizzas, including The People's Pizza-Deluxe (i.e., a "Supreme Pizza" at most other pizzerias), Philly Cheese Steak, Pacific Veggie, and more. The price on the mix-and-match deal may vary; it costs $10.99 per pizza here in Los Angeles. You can opt for Handmade Pan or Parmesan Stuffed Crust for an additional charge. Photo via Domino's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Denny's 2025 Holiday Menu Arrives
entry.summary:
Denny's 2025 holiday menu has arrived, anchored by the returning Rudolph Pancakes, which are themed after Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer.
The pancake is available on its own and as part of an Everyday Value Slam. An à la carte order of Rudolph Pancakes features two buttermilk pancakes arranged to make Rudolph's head decorated with turkey bacon antlers, whipped cream dollops, chocolate eyes, and a strawberry for Rudolph's red nose. The Rudolph Everyday Value Slam includes Rudolph Pancakes and two eggs. Prices vary, but the Slam is priced at $6.99 near me in the Los Angeles area, while the pancakes alone are $5. As a holiday promotion, Denny's Kids Eat Free deal is now available every day, from 4PM to 10PM, for a limited time. Under the terms of the offer, you can get a free Kids Meal with the purchase of each adult entree when dining in at participating locations nationwide. Other seasonal holiday items available now at Denny's include: - New Cinnamon Apple Crisp Pancake Slam - Two buttermilk pancakes cooked with cinnamon crumb topping, topped with warm apple crisp, salted caramel sauce, and powdered sugar. The pancakes are served with two eggs, hash browns, and two applewood-smoked bacon strips or two pork sausage links. - New Cold Brew Flavors - For a limited time, you can get cold brew coffee in three new seasonal flavors:
Salted Caramel - Cold brew mixed with milk and caramel syrup, poured over ice, and topped with whipped cream and caramel sauce. Peppermint Mocha - Cold brew mixed with milk and peppermint syrup, poured over ice, and topped with whipped cream and chocolate sauce. Sweet & Creamy - Cold brew mixed with milk and cane syrup, poured over ice.
- New Peppermint Hot Chocolate - Hot chocolate mixed with peppermint syrup and topped with whipped cream. Photo via Denny's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Dutch Bros Welcomes 2025 Holiday Menu
entry.summary:
Dutch Bros readies for the holidays with the release of its 2025 holiday menu, featuring new and returning seasonal drinks.
This year's holiday menu is available now at all Dutch Bros locations for a limited time and includes: - New Mistletoe Rebel - Raspberry and pomegranate flavors mixed with the chain's signature Rebel energy drink, and decorated with a green glitter glaze. You can get it blended, iced, or as a lemonade (a non-caffeinated option). - New Holiday Cookie Freeze - Espresso, sweet cream, and vanilla, swirled with jingle sprinkles and finished with Soft Top and more jingle sprinkles. You can also get it as a shake or iced latte. - New Jingle Nog Latte - Espresso and holiday nog, topped with Soft Top and jingle sprinkles. - New Jingle Chai Nog - Chai and holiday nog, topped with Soft Top and jingle sprinkles. - Hazelnut Truffle Mocha - A hazelnut mocha topped with Soft Top and a caramel drizzle. You can get it iced, hot, or as a Freeze. - Candy Cane Mocha - Espresso, peppermint flavor, and chocolate milk, crowned with Soft Top and candy cane sprinkles. You can get it iced, hot, as a Freeze, as a cold brew, or as a hot cocoa. This year's holiday drinks come in one of six holiday-themed cups. Iced holiday drinks also come with a limited-edition straw. Photo via Dutch Bros. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Krystal Introduces New Expanded Patty Melt Lineup
entry.summary:
Krystal builds upon its recently updated Classic Patty Melt with a new, limited-time expanded Patty Melt menu.
Here's what the lineup looks like (with suggested prices listed): - Classic Patty Melt ($5.79; also available in a combo for $9.79) - A 3-ounce burger patty, caramelized onions, and melted American cheese served on grilled, buttery toast. - New Chipotle Patty Melt ($6.79; also available in a combo for $10.79) - A 3-ounce burger patty, caramelized onions, melted American cheese, jalapenos, and Hellmann's Chipotle Mayo, served on grilled, buttery toast. - New Bacon & Egg Patty Melt (available all day for $6.99; also available in a combo for $10.99) - Bacon, a fried egg, American cheese, caramelized onions, and a 3-oz burger patty, served on grilled, buttery toast. - New Chipotle Bacon & Egg Patty Melt (available all day for $7.29; also available in a combo for $11.29) - Bacon, a fried egg, American cheese, caramelized onions, a 3-oz burger patty, jalapeno, and Hellmann's Chipotle Mayo, served on grilled, buttery toast. The new Patty Melts are available now for a limited time through January 11, 2026, at participating Krystal locations chain-wide. Photo via Krystal. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Crumbl Bakes Banana Pudding and More Through November 22, 2025
entry.summary:
Crumbl's weekly rotation of cookies and other desserts includes Banana Pudding and more this week.
Here's what's on deck at Crumbl for the week ending November 22, 2025 (I'm a bit surprised there isn't more of a Thanksgiving theme): - Banana Pudding - Layers of banana, vanilla pudding, and vanilla wafers, topped with whipped cream and vanilla wafer crumbs - Cookies & Creme Tres Leche Cake - A dark chocolate cake soaked in classic tres leches sauce, topped with whipped cream, and cookies & cream streusel. - Peanut Butter Cup Skillet Cookie featuring Reese's - A warm peanut butter cookie topped with peanut butter cup mousse, a drizzle of melted milk chocolate, and Reese's Peanut Butter Cup pieces. - Double Fudge Brownie Cookie - A warm brownie cookie topped with a chocolate glaze and brownie bites. - Blueberry Pancake Cookie - A warm blueberry pancake cookie topped with a blueberry buttermilk glaze and a dollop of buttercream. - Iced Oatmeal Cookie - A warm cinnamon oatmeal cookie covered in vanilla cream cheese glaze. - Milk Chocolate Chip Cookie - A warm cookie packed with milk chocolate chips. Crumbl also continues to offer a "Secret Menu," which is a cookie or other dessert chosen by each location. You can find what's being offered through the Crumbl website or mobile app. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Holiday Pie is Back at McDonald's
entry.summary:
The Holiday Pie is back at participating McDonald's restaurants nationwide for the 2025 holiday season.
The seasonal treat features a turnover-style, baked pie with a creamy custard encased in a buttery, sugar-glazed crust embedded with rainbow sprinkles. You can find my previous review of it here. Prices vary with location, but the Holiday Pie costs $2.49 or two for $4.25 at a local McDonald's here in Los Angeles. It replaced the Pumpkin and Creme Pie. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: Wendy's - Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion
entry.summary:
Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion features a Vanilla (or Chocolate) Frosty swirled with new snickerdoodle sauce and topped with snickerdoodle cookie crumbles.
It cost me $4.19 for a small, and I went with the vanilla version.
Unlike a McFlurry or a Blizzard, the Frosty Fusion is only mixed for a few seconds (if that). The sauce remained mostly drizzled on the sides and bottom of the cup, resulting in a mild cinnamon note to the creamy Vanilla Frosty and a much sweeter, more pronounced cinnamon taste whenever the sauce was encountered. Interestingly, the sauce contained visible flecks of cinnamon. The snickerdoodle cookie crumbles on top had the requisite mix of sweet vanilla and cinnamon flavors. They were super crunchy (although this experience may vary), providing a nice contrast to the smooth Frosty and liquid sauce.
Overall, I enjoyed Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion a good deal. I like snickerdoodles, and the flavor was captured well here. I especially liked the hearty crunch of the cookies on top as that can be hit-or-miss with this type of dessert. Nutritional Info - Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion Calories - 410 Fat - 11g (Saturated Fat - 6g) Sodium - 320mg Carbs - 70g (Sugar - 56g) Protein - 9g Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: McRib Returns to Select McDonald's Restaurants
entry.summary:
It's McRib season once again at McDonald's, but you can only find the barbecue sauce-slathered pork patty at participating locations in select regions.
Cities where the McRib is available for a limited time include Miami, Dallas, Cleveland, St. Louis, Atlanta, Chicago, Seattle, Los Angeles, and more. The McRib features a seasoned boneless pork patty shaped like a rack of ribs and slathered in barbecue sauce and served with slivered onions and pickles on a toasted cornmeal-dusted roll. Prices vary, but the McRib costs $6.29 at my local McDonald's in Los Angeles. The combo, which includes the sandwich, medium fries, and a medium drink, costs $11.29. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!

author.name: Brand Eating. Your Daily Fast Food Reading.

entry.title: Royals, Guardians Among Teams Interested In Brendan Donovan

entry.title: Shake Shack's 2025 Holiday Shakes Arrive
entry.summary:
Shake Shack introduces two seasonal shakes for the 2025 holiday season: the Christmas Cookie Shake and the Sticky Toffee Pudding Shake.
Available for a limited time, the shakes are inspired by traditional holiday desserts. The returning Christmas Cookie Shake features a sweet, buttery frozen custard blended with milk and topped with whipped cream and holiday sprinkles. The new Sticky Toffee Pudding Shake features sticky toffee pudding (a type of steamed cake) spun with vanilla frozen custard and caramelized brown sugar, topped with whipped cream and a drizzle of sticky toffee pudding sauce. Prices vary, but both holiday shakes cost $6.99 each here in the Los Angeles area. Photo via Shake Shack. Read more at Brand Eating!

- New York Cheesecake - A donut dipped in vanilla-flavored icing, topped with graham crunch, and filled with cheesecake-flavored Kreme. - Oreo Cookies and Kreme - A donut filled with Oreo cookies & Kreme filling, dipped in chocolate icing, topped with Oreo cookie pieces and a drizzle of vanilla-flavored icing. - Original Glazed Cake - An old-fashioned cake donut covered in the chain's signature Original Glaze. - Cinnamon Apple Filled - A donut filled with apple cinnamon filling and tossed in a powdered cinnamon-flavored coating. - Original Glazed Kreme Filled Donut - An Original Glazed donut filled with Kreme filling. - Biscoff…

entry.title: Checkers & Rally's Introduces New $5 Pretzel Pubster Combo
entry.summary:
Checkers & Rally's launches its new $5 Pretzel Pubster Combo as the chain's latest value meal.
The new Pretzel Pubster Burger features two beef patties, melted beer cheese with bacon crumbles, American cheese, mayo, and crispy onions, served on a toasted pretzel bun. The $5 meal also includes Famous Seasoned Fries and a small fountain drink (the burger by itself costs $3.99 near me in the Los Angeles area). The fast-food chain also offers a bigger take on a pretzel burger in the new BBQ Pretzel Buford. With a suggested price of $7.49, the burger features two large beef patties, American cheese, two bacon strips, crispy-fried onions, Sweet & Smoky BBQ sauce, and mayo, on a toasted pretzel bun. Checkers & Rally's is also offering Classic Wings for $1 each for the fall sports season. The bone-in wings are available plain or tossed in one of several sauces, including new Frank's Stingin' Honey Garlic Sauce, Medium Buffalo Sauce, Angry Buffalo Sauce, Garlic Parmesan Sauce, and Sweet & Smoky BBQ. Photo via Checkers & Rally's. Read more at Brand Eating!
